forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a080
+project: local-review
+title: 'Harden review module: path-traversal confinement, shared probe-evidence renderer, refuted_by rename, missing type docs'
+---
+## What
+
+Pre-existing review-module issues surfaced by the over-broad review of task 01KV3PJGP487TN46CH8JQD6RP7 (its `review/*.rs` glob swept the whole module, not its two changed files). These are real but were NOT introduced by that task — split here so they're tracked without blocking it. Ordered by severity.
+
+### Substantive
+- [ ] **Path-traversal confinement** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs` `read_text_file_under_repo` builds the read path from the agent's `fs/read_text_file` request with no confinement: `if req.path.is_absolute() { req.path.clone() } else { repo_root.join(&req.path) }` reads any absolute path verbatim, and a relative path with `..` segments escapes `repo_root` (the join is never canonicalized + `starts_with`-checked). The doc comment claims the read is "resolved under repo_root" but the code does not enforce it. Fix: reject (or relativize) absolute paths, `repo_root.join(&req.path)`, `canonicalize`, verify the canonical result `starts_with(canonical_repo_root)` before `read_to_string`, else return `invalid_params`. (Severity warning, not blocker: requester is the locally-run review LLM at user privilege — but the confinement the doc promises should hold.)
+- [ ] **Duplicate `render_probe_evidence`** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:432` re-implements the identical evidence renderer from `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:831` (row format `file_path :line `symbol` @ {sim:.2} — {detail}`, plus the `_No probe evidence._`/`(no rows)` sentinels) byte-for-byte; only divergence is the `({kind})` header annotation. Extract one shared `render_probe_evidence(out, results, show_kind: bool)` both stages import. (No duplicate-but-different code.)
+- [ ] **`refuted_by` misleading wire-contract name** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:299` writes confirmed findings with `refuted_by: Some(RefutingLayer::Agent)`, so a *confirmed* finding serializes as `{"confirmed": true, "refuted_by": "Agent"}` — name contradicts value, and `VerifiedFinding` derives Serialize/Deserialize (types.rs:116) so it's part of the wire contract. Rename to `decided_by`/`adjudicated_by` (serde rename) and fix the verify.rs module doc (lines ~28-29) to "which layer reached the verdict". `refuted_by.is_some()` must not be the "was refuted" test.
+- [ ] **`read_at_ref`/`read_working` swallow real errors** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:300` collapses every failure to `None` via `.ok()`, conflating "path absent at this ref" (the intended Added/Deleted signal) with genuine failures (permission, non-UTF8/binary blob) — a binary/unreadable tracked file silently diffs as wholly added/removed. Map only the genuine not-found case to `None`; propagate other errors as `AvpError::Context`.
+
+### Refactor-altitude (lower priority; address only if cheap)
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:214` — `scope_review` ~95 lines / six stages; extract `group_entities_by_file`, `match_validators`, `compute_file_facts`, `assemble_validator_work`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:230` — `run_validator_fleet` ~61 lines / three phases; extract `submit_validator_batches` + `collect_validator_batches`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — `bounded_slice` 4-level nesting; flatten with `let ... else`/early-continue or an `entity_sections` helper.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:118` — `synthesize` ~55 lines; extract the per-severity `render_sections` loop.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/types.rs:1` — `extract_json_value` four nested strategies; extract `from_json_fence`/`from_bare_fence`/`from_balanced`/`from_outer` and chain with `.or_else`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:40` — `FleetTally::new(attempted, failed)` two adjacent usizes transpose silently; drop `new` for the named struct literal.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:60` — hoist `const SCOPE_VALIDATOR: &str = "scope"` (3 hand-written `"scope"` literals).
+
+### Missing type-level docs (nits)
+- [ ] Add `///` type docs: `FleetConfig` (fleet.rs:30), `FleetOutcome` (fleet.rs:47), `ProbeOp` (probes.rs:37), `CATALOG` (probes.rs:63), `ChangeEntry` (probes.rs:86), `FileChange` (probes.rs:105), `ProbeRow` (probes.rs:118), `ProbeResult` (probes.rs:140), `VerifyOutcome` (verify.rs), `Candidate` (verify.rs:21).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `read_text_file_under_repo` rejects `..`-escape and absolute-path reads outside `repo_root` (canonicalize + `starts_with` check), returning `invalid_params`; legitimate in-repo reads still work.
+- [ ] One shared `render_probe_evidence`; no second copy in verify.rs.
+- [ ] The verdict-layer field is renamed to reflect "who decided", with serde rename so the wire contract is corrected; module docs match.
+- [ ] `read_at_ref`/`read_working` distinguish not-found from real errors.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` and `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `drive.rs` unit test: `read_text_file_under_repo` with `../../etc/passwd` and an absolute outside path → error; an in-repo relative path → Ok.
+- [ ] Test asserting fleet and verify probe-evidence render identically through the shared renderer (with/without the kind annotation).
+- [ ] Serde round-trip test asserting a confirmed finding does not serialize a "refuted"-named field with a truthy value.
+- [ ] `scope.rs` test: a not-found path yields None; an unreadable/binary blob is not silently treated as added/deleted.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file

These are real but were NOT introduced by that task — split here so they're tracked without blocking it. Ordered by severity.
-
-### Substantive
-- [ ] **Path-traversal confinement** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs` `read_text_file_under_repo` builds the read path from the agent's `fs/read_text_file` request with no confinement: `if req.path.is_absolute() { req.path.clone() } else { repo_root.join(&req.path) }` reads any absolute path verbatim, and a relative path with `..` segments escapes `repo_root` (the join is never canonicalized + `starts_with`-checked). Fix: reject (or relativize) absolute paths, `repo_root.join(&req.path)`, `canonicalize`, verify the canonical result `starts_with(canonical_repo_root)` before `read_to_string`, else return `invalid_params`. (Severity warning, not blocker: requester is the locally-run review LLM at user privilege — but the confinement the doc promises should hold.)

Extract one shared `render_probe_evidence(out, results, show_kind: bool)` both stages import. (No duplicate-but-different code.)
-- [ ] **`refuted_by` misleading wire-contract name** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:299` writes confirmed findings with `refuted_by: Some(RefutingLayer::Agent)`, so a *confirmed* finding serializes as `{"confirmed": true, "refuted_by": "Agent"}` — name contradicts value, and `VerifiedFinding` derives Serialize/Deserialize (types.rs:116) so it's part of the wire contract. -- [ ] **`read_at_ref`/`read_working` swallow real errors** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:300` collapses every failure to `None` via `.ok()`, conflating "path absent at this ref" (the intended Added/Deleted signal) with genuine failures (permission, non-UTF8/binary blob) — a binary/unreadable tracked file silently diffs as wholly added/removed. -
-### Missing type-level docs (nits)

These are real but were NOT introduced by that task — split here so they're tracked without blocking it. Ordered by severity.

 ### Substantive
-- [ ] **Path-traversal confinement** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs` `read_text_file_under_repo` builds the read path from the agent's `fs/read_text_file` request with no confinement: `if req.path.is_absolute() { req.path.clone() } else { repo_root.join(&req.path) }` reads any absolute path verbatim, and a relative path with `..` segments escapes `repo_root` (the join is never canonicalized + `starts_with`-checked). Fix: reject (or relativize) absolute paths, `repo_root.join(&req.path)`, `canonicalize`, verify the canonical result `starts_with(canonical_repo_root)` before `read_to_string`, else return `invalid_params`. (Severity warning, not blocker: requester is the locally-run review LLM at user privilege — but the confinement the doc promises should hold.)

-- [ ] **`refuted_by` misleading wire-contract name** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:299` writes confirmed findings with `refuted_by: Some(RefutingLayer::Agent)`, so a *confirmed* finding serializes as `{"confirmed": true, "refuted_by": "Agent"}` — name contradicts value, and `VerifiedFinding` derives Serialize/Deserialize (types.rs:116) so it's part of the wire contract. Rename to `decided_by`/`adjudicated_by` (serde rename) and fix the verify.rs module doc (lines ~28-29) to "which layer reached the verdict". `refuted_by.is_some()` must not be the "was refuted" test.
-- [ ] **`read_at_ref`/`read_working` swallow real errors** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:300` collapses every failure to `None` via `.ok()`, conflating "path absent at this ref" (the intended Added/Deleted signal) with genuine failures (permission, non-UTF8/binary blob) — a binary/unreadable tracked file silently diffs as wholly added/removed. Map only the genuine not-found case to `None`; propagate other errors as `AvpError::Context`.
+- [x] **Path-traversal confinement** — `drive.rs` `read_text_file_under_repo` now confines via new `confine_under_repo`: canonicalizes `repo_root` + the candidate, requires the canonical target `starts_with` the canonical root, else returns an error mapped to `invalid_params`. Absolute-but-in-repo paths still honored (boundary is location, not shape). Doc comment corrected to describe the enforced confinement.
+- [x] **Duplicate `render_probe_evidence`** — extracted one shared `pub(crate) render_probe_evidence(out, results, show_kind)` in `probes.rs`; `fleet.rs` (show_kind=false) and `verify.rs` (show_kind=true) both call it; the two byte-for-byte copies are gone.
+- [x] **`refuted_by` misleading wire-contract name** — renamed `VerifiedFinding::refuted_by` → `decided_by` (changes the wire key), updated all writers/readers + the verify.rs module doc to "which layer reached the verdict"; added doc noting `decided_by.is_some()` is NOT the "was refuted" test.
+- [x] **`read_at_ref`/`read_working` swallow real errors** — both now return `Result<Option<String>, AvpError>`: only genuine not-found maps to `Ok(None)` (the Added/Deleted signal); permission errors and binary/non-UTF8 blobs propagate as `AvpError::Context`. Callers updated. `git2` promoted to a direct (non-optional) dep so the `git2::ErrorCode::NotFound` discrimination compiles.

 ### Refactor-altitude (lower priority; address only if cheap)
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:214` — `scope_review` ~95 lines / six stages; extract `group_entities_by_file`, `match_validators`, `compute_file_facts`, `assemble_validator_work`.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:230` — `run_validator_fleet` ~61 lines / three phases; extract `submit_validator_batches` + `collect_validator_batches`.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — `bounded_slice` 4-level nesting; flatten with `let ... else`/early-continue or an `entity_sections` helper.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:118` — `synthesize` ~55 lines; extract the per-severity `render_sections` loop.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/types.rs:1` — `extract_json_value` four nested strategies; extract `from_json_fence`/`from_bare_fence`/`from_balanced`/`from_outer` and chain with `.or_else`.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:40` — `FleetTally::new(attempted, failed)` two adjacent usizes transpose silently; drop `new` for the named struct literal.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:60` — hoist `const SCOPE_VALIDATOR: &str = "scope"` (3 hand-written `"scope"` literals).
+- [x] `scope.rs` — hoisted `const SCOPE_VALIDATOR: &str = "scope"` (3 hand-written `"scope"` literals).
+- [ ] `scope.rs:214` — `scope_review` extract helpers. (deferred — not cheap, scope creep)
+- [ ] `fleet.rs:230` — `run_validator_fleet` extract submit/collect. (deferred)
+- [ ] `scope.rs` — `bounded_slice` flatten. (deferred)
+- [ ] `synthesize.rs:118` — `synthesize` extract render_sections. (deferred)
+- [ ] `types.rs:1` — `extract_json_value` extract strategies. (deferred)
+- [ ] `synthesize.rs:40` — drop `FleetTally::new`. (deferred — touches many shared-file test call sites)

 ### Missing type-level docs (nits)
-- [ ] Add `///` type docs: `FleetConfig` (fleet.rs:30), `FleetOutcome` (fleet.rs:47), `ProbeOp` (probes.rs:37), `CATALOG` (probes.rs:63), `ChangeEntry` (probes.rs:86), `FileChange` (probes.rs:105), `ProbeRow` (probes.rs:118), `ProbeResult` (probes.rs:140), `VerifyOutcome` (verify.rs), `Candidate` (verify.rs:21).
+- [x] Verified: `FleetConfig`, `FleetOutcome`, `ProbeOp`, `CATALOG`, `ChangeEntry`, `FileChange`, `ProbeRow`, `ProbeResult`, `VerifyOutcome`, `Candidate` all ALREADY carry `///` type docs (task line numbers were stale). No action needed.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `read_text_file_under_repo` rejects `..`-escape and absolute-path reads outside `repo_root` (canonicalize + `starts_with` check), returning `invalid_params`; legitimate in-repo reads still work.
-- [ ] One shared `render_probe_evidence`; no second copy in verify.rs.
-- [ ] The verdict-layer field is renamed to reflect "who decided", with serde rename so the wire contract is corrected; module docs match.
-- [ ] `read_at_ref`/`read_working` distinguish not-found from real errors.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` and `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
+- [x] `read_text_file_under_repo` rejects `..`-escape and absolute-path reads outside `repo_root` (canonicalize + `starts_with` check), returning an invalid_params error; legitimate in-repo reads still work.
+- [x] One shared `render_probe_evidence`; no second copy in verify.rs.
+- [x] The verdict-layer field is renamed to reflect "who decided" (`decided_by`); wire contract corrected; module docs match.
+- [x] `read_at_ref`/`read_working` distinguish not-found from real errors.
+- [x] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` (301 passed) and `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators -p swissarmyhammer-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `drive.rs` unit test: `read_text_file_under_repo` with `../../etc/passwd` and an absolute outside path → error; an in-repo relative path → Ok.
-- [ ] Test asserting fleet and verify probe-evidence render identically through the shared renderer (with/without the kind annotation).
-- [ ] Serde round-trip test asserting a confirmed finding does not serialize a "refuted"-named field with a truthy value.
-- [ ] `scope.rs` test: a not-found path yields None; an unreadable/binary blob is not silently treated as added/deleted.

+- [x] `probes.rs`: shared renderer with/without kind annotation + empty/no-rows sentinels.
+- [x] `types.rs`: serde test asserting a confirmed finding does NOT serialize a `refuted_by` field and uses `decided_by`.
+- [x] `scope.rs`: not-found path → Ok(None); binary/non-UTF8 working file and committed blob → AvpError::Context (not silently added/deleted).

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Used `/tdd` (RED→GREEN for each substantive item).
\ No newline at end of file

+- [ ] **Duplicate `render_probe_evidence`** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:432` re-implements the identical evidence renderer from `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:831` (row format `file_path :line `symbol` @ {sim:.2} — {detail}`, plus the `_No probe evidence._`/`(no rows)` sentinels) byte-for-byte; only divergence is the `({kind})` header annotation. Extract one shared `render_probe_evidence(out, results, show_kind: bool)` both stages import. (No duplicate-but-different code.)
+- [ ] **`refuted_by` misleading wire-contract name** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:299` writes confirmed findings with `refuted_by: Some(RefutingLayer::Agent)`, so a *confirmed* finding serializes as `{"confirmed": true, "refuted_by": "Agent"}` — name contradicts value, and `VerifiedFinding` derives Serialize/Deserialize (types.rs:116) so it's part of the wire contract. Rename to `decided_by`/`adjudicated_by` (serde rename) and fix the verify.rs module doc (lines ~28-29) to "which layer reached the verdict". `refuted_by.is_some()` must not be the "was refuted" test.
+- [ ] **`read_at_ref`/`read_working` swallow real errors** — `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:300` collapses every failure to `None` via `.ok()`, conflating "path absent at this ref" (the intended Added/Deleted signal) with genuine failures (permission, non-UTF8/binary blob) — a binary/unreadable tracked file silently diffs as wholly added/removed. Map only the genuine not-found case to `None`; propagate other errors as `AvpError::Context`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [x] `read_text_file_under_repo` rejects `..`-escape and absolute-path reads outside `repo_root` (canonicalize + `starts_with` check), returning an invalid_params error; legitimate in-repo reads still work.

-- [x] The verdict-layer field is renamed to reflect "who decided" (`decided_by`); wire contract corrected; module docs match.
-- [x] `read_at_ref`/`read_working` distinguish not-found from real errors.
